chapter_title.en: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible for the Muhrim to Do and Use of All Types

text: عَبْدُ اَللَّهِ بْنُ سِنَانٍ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : أَ رَأَيْتَ إِنْ وَجَدْتُ عَلَيَّ قُرَاداً أَوْ حَلَمَةً أَطْرَحُهَا عَنِّي وَ أَنَا مُحْرِمٌ قَالَ «نَعَمْ وَ صَغَاراً لَهُمَا إِنَّهُمَا رَقِيَا فِي غَيْرِ مَرْقَاهُمَا ».

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.2698 - Abdullah ibn Sinan asked Abu Abdullah (as): "What do you say if I find a tick or a louse on me while I am in the state of ihram? Should I remove it?"
Imam (as) said: "Yes, even the small ones of these, as they have ascended to a place where they do not belong."
